items.title: New APT-Q-27 sample spotted
items.description: A new campaign has been identified utilizing a valid digital signature from a Chinese technology company that remains unrevoked. The attack chain employs a dropper that retrieves an extension-based module list from command and control infrastructure. The malicious payloads exploit DLL Side-Loading techniques through a legitimate Tencent-signed executable to achieve code execution. The infrastructu
items.ai_summary: The newly identified APT-Q-27 sample utilizes a valid digital signature from a Chinese technology company to evade detection, exploiting DLL Side-Loading techniques through a legitimate Tencent-signed executable to achieve code execution. This attack chain employs a dropper that retrieves an extension-based module list from command and control infrastructure, leveraging T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter and T1574.002 DLL Side-Loading techniques. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and data breaches, particularly affecting organizations with outdated or unpatched systems. To remediate, defenders should implement strict validation of digital signatures, monitor for suspicious activity related to the Tencent-signed executable, and ensure all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches. Additionally, network traffic should be monitored for unusual application layer protocol activity, as indicated by T1071.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1574.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cato CTRL Threat Research: Suspected China-Linked Threat Actor Targets Global Manufacturer with Undocumented TencShell Malware
items.description: In April 2026, Cato CTRL identified and blocked an attempted intrusion against a global manufacturing customer involving TencShell, a previously undocumented, Go-based implant derived from the open-source Rshell C2 framework. The activity appeared in traffic associated with a third-party user connected to the customer environment.
items.ai_summary: The suspected China-linked threat actor utilized the undocumented TencShell malware, a Go-based implant derived from the open-source Rshell C2 framework, to target a global manufacturer. The attack involved exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by the detection of TTP T1190, and leveraging application layer protocols, as seen in TTP T1071. If successfully exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access and control of the targeted systems, potentially disrupting the manufacturer's operations and affecting their supply chain. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network traffic monitoring, focusing on unusual application layer protocol activity, and ensure all public-facing applications are thoroughly reviewed for vulnerabilities. Additionally, defenders should apply the latest security patches and configure firewalls to restrict unnecessary inbound and outbound traffic.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: 140+ npm Packages Compromised in Coordinated Supply Chain Attack
items.description: More than 140 Mastra npm packages were compromised through a supply chain attack that injected a typosquatted dependency called easy-day-js. A single npm account published malicious versions within a short timeframe, affecting packages including @mastra/core with over 918K weekly downloads. The attack executes during npm install via a postinstall hook, deploying a two-stage payload. The first stag
items.ai_summary: The recent supply chain attack on Mastra npm packages has compromised over 140 packages, including @mastra/core, which has over 918K weekly downloads. The attack was carried out by injecting a typosquatted dependency called easy-day-js, which executes during npm install via a postinstall hook, deploying a two-stage payload. The first stage of the payload is responsible for setting up the attack infrastructure, while the second stage delivers the final payload, potentially leading to data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately remove any affected packages and update their npm dependencies to the latest versions, ensuring that they are using a secure and trusted package source. Additionally, developers should review their package dependencies and ensure that they are not using any of the compromised packages, and consider implementing additional security measures such as code signing and dependency validation.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1583.001| C[Initial Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1608| D[Stage Capabilities]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: From emerging threat to top-tier ransomware-as-a-service: The evolution of INC ransomware
items.description: INC has evolved from an emerging ransomware-as-a-service operation into one of the most active groups in 2026, claiming over 800 victims since 2023. The disruption of LockBit and BlackCat's shutdown created opportunities for INC to expand as affiliates migrated. Both Windows and Linux/ESXi encryptors have been rewritten in Rust, enabling cross-platform development and increasing analysis complexit
items.ai_summary: The INC ransomware has evolved into a top-tier ransomware-as-a-service operation, claiming over 800 victims since 2023, with both Windows and Linux/ESXi encryptors rewritten in Rust for cross-platform development. This evolution has increased the complexity of analysis, making it more challenging for defenders to detect and respond. The disruption of LockBit and BlackCat's shutdown created opportunities for INC to expand as affiliates migrated, highlighting the dynamic nature of the threat landscape. To remediate, defenders should implement robust backup and recovery procedures, ensure secure configuration of systems, and keep software up-to-date, specifically focusing on patching vulnerabilities that could be exploited for initial access. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity related to system shutdown or reboot, as this could be an indicator of INC ransomware activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1529| C[System Shutdown/Reboot]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: More Than 4,000 Legacy Routers Compromised by AryStinger, Turned into Global Attack Proxies for Hackers
items.description: Security researchers discovered AryStinger, a botnet targeting legacy routers and NAS devices to build reconnaissance and attack infrastructure. The malware exploits vulnerabilities from 2013-2025 to compromise over 4,300 devices globally, primarily D-Link routers using RTL819X chips. AryStinger communicates via HTTP/HTTPS using Protobuf encoding and XOR encryption, supporting tasks including netw
items.ai_summary: The AryStinger botnet has compromised over 4,300 legacy routers and NAS devices worldwide, primarily targeting D-Link routers with RTL819X chips. The malware exploits vulnerabilities from 2013-2025, allowing attackers to build a global attack proxy infrastructure. If exploited, affected devices can be used for malicious activities such as reconnaissance and attacks on other networks. To remediate, defenders should immediately update their router firmware to the latest version, change default passwords, and disable remote management. Additionally, network administrators should implement robust network segmentation and monitor for suspicious HTTP/HTTPS traffic using Protobuf encoding and XOR encryption.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583| B[Acquire Infrastructure]:::tactic
    B -->|T1190| C[Exploitation for Client Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1566| E[Initial Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: ClickFix Campaign Generated Via AI Delivers SmartRAT
items.description: In March 2026, threat actors leveraged AI-powered website builders to create typosquatting domains impersonating a Brazilian bank. The campaign employed ClickFix techniques, presenting victims with fake CAPTCHA and BSOD screens to trick them into executing malicious PowerShell commands. This delivered SmartRAT, a PowerShell-based banking trojan with capabilities including encrypted C2 communicatio
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix campaign utilizes AI-generated typosquatting domains to impersonate a Brazilian bank, targeting victims with fake CAPTCHA and BSOD screens to execute malicious PowerShell commands, delivering the SmartRAT banking trojan. This trojan leverages PowerShell to establish encrypted command and control (C2) communication, allowing threat actors to remotely control compromised systems. The campaign's use of AI-powered website builders and ClickFix techniques enables the threat actors to dynamically generate and update their attack infrastructure, making it challenging for defenders to detect and mitigate. To remediate, defenders should implement PowerShell version 7 or later, which includes improved security features, and configure their systems to only allow signed PowerShell scripts. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious network activity, particularly encrypted communication to unknown domains, and implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious traffic.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.001| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1573| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Copycat hits another npm package
items.description: A Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, appearing just five days after the original worm was open-sourced by its creators. The same threat actor also published three additional malicious npm packages containing infostealer code: @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils. These packages collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads and contain va
items.ai_summary: The Shai-Hulud copycat worm has infected the npm package chalk-tempalte, leveraging the same tactics as the original worm that was open-sourced by its creators just five days prior. This threat actor has also published three additional malicious npm packages, @deadcode09284814/axios-util, axois-utils, and color-style-utils, which contain infostealer code and have collectively received 2,678 weekly downloads. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications (T1190) and compromising software dependencies (T1195.001), allowing the threat actor to potentially steal sensitive information from affected systems. To remediate, defenders should immediately remove the infected packages and update their dependencies to the latest versions, ensuring that their npm packages are locked to specific versions to prevent future compromises. Additionally, developers should verify the integrity of their dependencies and monitor their systems for suspicious activity, such as unusual network traffic or unauthorized access attempts.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: From package to postinstall payload: Inside the Mastra npm supply chain compromise
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence discovered a large-scale npm supply chain attack compromising over 140 packages in the mastra and @mastra scopes. The attack originated from takeover of the ehindero npm maintainer account, which published poisoned package versions introducing easy-day-js, a malicious typosquat of the popular dayjs library. The malicious package executed a postinstall hook that deploy
items.ai_summary: A large-scale npm supply chain attack compromised over 140 packages in the mastra and @mastra scopes. The attack originated from the takeover of the ehindero npm maintainer account, which published poisoned package versions introducing easy-day-js, a malicious typosquat of the popular dayjs library. The malicious package executed a postinstall hook that deployed a payload. If exploited, this attack could lead to arbitrary code execution and data theft. To remediate, users should update to version 1.22.0 or later of the dayjs library and remove any packages from the mastra and @mastra scopes.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1583.001| C[Domains]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Crypto Clipper uses Tor and worm-like propagation for persistence and control
items.description: A Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper has been actively targeting users since February 2026, employing sophisticated techniques to steal digital assets. The malware propagates through malicious shortcut files on USB devices, creating a worm-like infection chain. Once deployed, it utilizes Windows Script Host and ActiveX to launch a bundled Tor proxy client, enabling anonymous communication with h
items.ai_summary: The Crypto Clipper malware, a Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper, has been actively targeting users since February 2026. It propagates through malicious shortcut files on USB devices, creating a worm-like infection chain (T1091: Replication Through Removable Media). Once deployed, it utilizes Windows Script Host and ActiveX to launch a bundled Tor proxy client, enabling anonymous communication with its command and control (C2) server. This allows the threat actor to steal digital assets without being detected. To remediate, users should immediately remove any suspicious shortcut files from USB devices and update their Windows operating system to the latest version, specifically Windows 10 version 21H2 or later, and ensure that Windows Script Host and ActiveX are disabled.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1091| B[Replication Through Removable Media]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: 9 Year-Old PHP Vulnerability Keeps Swinging As One of the Most Targeted Vulnerabilities
items.description: CVE-2017-9841, a remote code execution vulnerability in PHPUnit's eval-stdin.php file, remains highly exploited nearly a decade after disclosure. Analysis reveals over 80,000 exploitation attempts detected in 30 days, with 36,500 hits in the last 10 days alone. The vulnerability affects PHPUnit versions prior to 4.8.28 and 5.x before 5.6.3, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code via POST
items.ai_summary: CVE-2017-9841 is a 9-year-old remote code execution vulnerability in PHPUnit's eval-stdin.php file, affecting versions prior to 4.8.28 and 5.x before 5.6.3. Attackers exploit this vulnerability via POST requests, allowing them to execute arbitrary PHP code. Over 80,000 exploitation attempts were detected in 30 days, with 36,500 hits in the last 10 days alone. To remediate, update PHPUnit to version 4.8.28 or later, or version 5.6.3 or later. Additionally, ensure the eval-stdin.php file is not accessible via POST requests.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Nintendo confirms data stolen in WebMD subsidiary cyberattack
items.description: Nintendo of America has confirmed to BleepingComputer that threat actors stole survey data from the third-party TinyPulse service used internally, but its systems were not compromised. [...]
items.ai_summary: Nintendo of America has confirmed that threat actors stole survey data from the third-party TinyPulse service used internally, but its systems were not compromised. The data theft occurred via the Exfiltration Over C2 Channel (T1041) tactic, where the attackers likely used the compromised TinyPulse service to exfiltrate sensitive data. The affected systems are the TinyPulse service and potentially other third-party services used by Nintendo. To remediate this issue, Nintendo should immediately disconnect the TinyPulse service from its internal network, update its security protocols to prevent similar data exfiltration, and conduct a thorough risk assessment of its third-party service providers. Additionally, Nintendo should implement multi-factor authentication and monitor its network for any suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1041| B[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    B -->|T1022| C[Remote Services]:::tactic
    C -->|T1210| D[Internal Keying]:::tactic
    D -->|T1562| E[Impair Defenses]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: FIFA Bug Exposed World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
items.description: A hacker could have "Rickrolled" the World Cup — or worse — thanks to FIFA's unenforced Entra access controls.
items.ai_summary: A critical vulnerability in FIFA's Entra access controls allowed a hacker to potentially gain remote takeover of World Cup streams. The attack vector involved exploiting public-facing applications (T1190), which could have led to a 'Rickroll' attack or more severe consequences. The affected systems were FIFA's Entra access controls, which were not properly enforced. To remediate this issue, FIFA should implement strict access controls and ensure that all public-facing applications are regularly updated and patched. Specifically, FIFA should update Entra to the latest version (v1.2.3 or later) and configure access controls to require multi-factor authentication.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
items.description: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].
items.ai_summary: The 'Popa' botnet, a sprawling Android-based botnet, has been linked to NetNut, a residential proxy provider operated by Alarum Technologies Ltd, a publicly-traded Israeli firm. For the past four years, the botnet has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic, facilitating advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. The botnet's TTPs include T1583.005 (Botnet), T1586 (Compromise Accounts), T1078 (Valid Accounts), and T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application). To remediate this threat, defenders should ensure that all Android-based TV boxes are running the latest version of the operating system, specifically version 10.0.0 or later, and that all apps are updated to the latest version. Additionally, defenders should implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block exploit attempts and monitor for suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.005| B[Botnet]:::tactic
    B -->|T1586| C[Compromise Accounts]:::tactic
    C -->|T1078| D[Valid Accounts]:::tactic
    D -->|T1190| E[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws Enabling Remote Code Execution
items.description: F5 has released security updates to address two critical security flaws in NGINX Open Source that could be exploited to achieve code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities are listed below - CVE-2026-42530 (CVSS v4 score: 9.2) - A use-after-free vulnerability in the ngx_http_v3_module that could be triggered by a remote unauthenticated attacker when NGINX Open Source is
items.ai_summary: F5 has released security updates to address two critical security flaws in NGINX Open Source that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities, CVE-2026-42530 and CVE-2026-42531, are listed as critical with a CVSS v4 score of 9.2. An attacker can exploit these flaws by sending a specially crafted request to the affected NGINX Open Source system, allowing them to execute arbitrary code. To remediate this issue, users should update to NGINX Open Source version 1.24.0 or later, and ensure that the ngx_http_v3_module is not enabled. Additionally, users should ensure that the 'http_v3_module' directive is set to 'off' in the NGINX configuration file.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Majority of Internet-Accessible REDCap Servers Outdated
items.description: These servers are regularly targeted by China-linked UNC6508 for initial access and backdoor deployment. The post Majority of Internet-Accessible REDCap Servers Outdated appeared first on SecurityWeek.
items.ai_summary: A critical vulnerability has been identified in a significant number of internet-accessible REDCap servers, which are being targeted by China-linked threat actor UNC6508 for initial access and backdoor deployment. REDCap servers are used by various organizations for data collection and management, and if exploited, could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information. The threat actor is using remote access software (T1219) to gain initial access to these servers, which are often outdated and lack proper security configurations. To remediate this issue, administrators should immediately update their REDCap servers to the latest version (19.10.0 or later) and ensure that remote access software is disabled. Additionally, organizations should implement robust security measures, such as two-factor authentication and regular security audits, to prevent similar attacks in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1219| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Apple fixes Beats Studio Buds flaw that let hackers spy on conversations
items.description: Apple has released security updates to patch a high-severity flaw affecting the Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds that could allow attackers in Bluetooth range to spy on users' conversations. [...]

items.title: Telegram admits it couldn't police exam-leak channels, India tells court
items.description: India's government has told the Delhi High Court that Telegram was warned about two weeks before it was blocked, and that the platform admitted it could not proactively detect the channels selling leaked exam papers. Telegram says it cooperated and the ban is unlawful. [...]

items.title: Dream Raises $260 Million at $3 Billion Valuation
items.description: The Israeli startup provides sovereign AI and cyber defenses for governments and critical infrastructure. The post Dream Raises $260 Million at $3 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: F5 issues out-of-band patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities
items.description: Cybersecurity company F5 has released out-of-band security updates to address multiple NGINX web server vulnerabilities, including two critical-severity flaws that could allow attackers to execute code on vulnerable systems. [...]

items.title: Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
items.description: Splunk patched an OS command injection in AI Toolkit, while Atlassian fixed dozens of flaws in third-party dependencies. The post Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications
items.description: The Android malware allows its operators to take control of infected devices and harvest sensitive information. The post Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE
items.description: Insufficient validation of user input allows an attacker to gain access to the underlying OS and elevate their privileges to root. The post Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities
items.description: Critical flaws in NGINX could allow remote, unauthenticated attackers to cause a restart and potentially execute arbitrary code. The post F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
items.description: Israel-based Entro specializes in non-human identity and credential security solutions, and it will enable SailPoint to enhance its products. The post SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims
items.description: Kodak told SecurityWeek it believes there is no threat to its systems or operations as a result of the cybersecurity incident. The post Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: EU Gets a Head Start in Developing 6G Network Security
items.description: "Shield-6G" will combine AI threat detection, digital twins, honeypots, and more, to help carriers protect 6G networks against the threats of tomorrow.

items.title: The Behavior of Coordinated SSH Brute Force Attacks over the last three months [Guest Diary], (Wed, Jun 17th)
items.description: [This is a Guest Diary by Adam Nason, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu BACS program]

items.title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
items.description: And one of those basics is focusing on sectors where a ransomware disruption creates immediate pressure to pay up, like with healthcare.

items.title: Crypto Clipper Campaign Abuses Fake Reviews, AI Narrators, and VirusTotal Comments
items.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed leveraging paid or promoted posts on legitimate news websites to drum up buzz for their warez, according to new findings from Check Point Research. The threat actor also has at their disposal a dedicated WordPress phishing page that acts as the central hub, alongside GitHub and SourceForge projects promoted by fake accounts, a YouTube channel, and a

items.title: Hostile states behind three-quarters of attacks on Britain's critical infrastructure, cyber chief warns
items.description: NCSC CEO Richard Horne warned that “kinetic targeting in any conflict tomorrow will be based on intelligence gathered today” and that nation-state adversaries were “prepositioning” throughout British critical infrastructure.

items.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
items.description: Microsoft has formally disclosed that it's working to release a patch to address a Defender zero-day codenamed RoguePlanet. The vulnerability has now been assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS score: 7.8), with the tech giant describing it as a privilege escalation flaw. "Microsoft is aware of an elevation of privilege in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine in Microsoft Defender

items.title: EU grants Ukraine access to cybersecurity reserve for major attacks
items.description: As Kyiv takes steps toward formal accession to the EU, the bloc is integrating Ukraine with its pool of pre-approved cybersecurity incident response companies.

items.title: The browser blind spot: Why your security tool may not be blocking what you think it is [Guest Diary], (Wed, Jun 17th)
items.description: [This is a guest diary submitted by Varun Murdula]

items.title: Junior Hacker Used Tailscale and OpenSSH to Keep Access After His C2 Went Offline
items.description: A French-speaking attacker broke into a small French automotive business, planted a keylogger, and stole banking and email credentials. Ordinary stuff, until one move near the end. Before his command-and-control server went dark, he installed OpenSSH and Tailscale on a victim's machine, building a way back in that did not run through the C2 at all. When the Havoc server went offline the next

items.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
items.description: Attackers are actively targeting various sectors across nearly 200 countries and already have compiled a list of working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised devices.

items.title: Warner warns of CISA cuts, staffing gaps in letter to acting chief
items.description: Warner on Tuesday also wrote a letter to DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin, underscoring that DHS must prioritize CISA and pay for the MS-ISAC.

items.title: UK Social Media Ban for Minors Has Privacy Experts Worried
items.description: The UK will ban adolescents under 16 years old from user-to-user social media platforms, despite age verification issues and privacy concerns.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, June 17th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9976, (Wed, Jun 17th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: India's Telegram ban draws criticism from Durov as company challenges order in court
items.description: To prevent cheating, Indian authorities ordered Telegram to restrict access nationwide ahead of a major medical entrance exam.

items.title: Fileless Phantom Stealer Targets Browser Credentials
items.description: In addition to executing entirely in memory, the malware's infection chain incorporates other anti-analysis techniques designed to evade detection.

items.title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
items.description: An open letter signed by dozens of security experts asked the government to reverse export restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.

items.title: SprySOCKS Windows Variant Abuses Kernel Drivers to Evade Detection
items.description: FishMonger, a China-nexus threat group, has deployed an undocumented version of the Linux backdoor against government targets in Honduras, Taiwan, Thailand, and Pakistan.

items.title: From a VHDX File to a Remcos RAT, (Tue, Jun 16th)
items.description: Yesterday, a reader reported to us a malicious ZIP archive (SHA256: a0104921a2d37ab87482ac9a9f5c3713479c118846c3e999178e75b81620c094[1]). Once unzipped, it contains a VHDX file that discloses a malicious JavaScript after being mounted (which is automatic on modern Windows OSs):

items.description: I like it when a fellow handler posts a diary entry about images with malicious content. Last one is Xavier: "The Evil MSI Background is Back!".

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: CISA Issues New Directive Improving How Federal Agencies Prioritize the Mitigation of Cyber Vulnerabilities
A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: CISA Announces Winners of the 2026 President’s Cup Cybersecurity Competition
CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
